sentences.text: The RVA22 profiles are intended to be used for 64-bit application processors running rich OS stacks.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles

sentences.text: Only user-mode (RVA22U64) and supervisor-mode (RVA22S64) profiles are specified in this family.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles

sentences.text: The RVA22U64 profile specifies the ISA features available to user-mode execution environments in 64-bit applications processors.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile

sentences.text: This is the most important profile within the application processor family in terms of the amount of software that targets this profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVA22U64 and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: As per the unprivileged architecture specification, the `ecall` instruction causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The following mandatory extensions were present in RVA20U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: extlink:m[] Integer multiplication and division. - extlink:a[] Atomic instructions. - Single-precision floating-point instructions. - Double-precision floating-point instructions. - extlink:c[] Compressed Instructions. - extlink:zicsr[] CSR instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: These are implied by presence of F. - Base counters and timers.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support instruction fetch, and any instruction fetches of naturally aligned power-of-2 sizes up to min(ILEN,XLEN) (i.e., 32 bits for RVA22) are atomic.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support RsrvEventual.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support AMOArithmetic.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Misaligned loads and stores to main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Even though mandated, misaligned loads and stores might execute extremely slowly.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Standard software distributions should assume their existence only for correctness, not for performance.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The following mandatory feature was further restricted in RVA22U64:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Reservation sets are contiguous, naturally aligned, and a maximum of 64 bytes.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The maximum reservation size has been reduced to match the required cache block size.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The minimum reservation size is effectively set by the instructions in the mandatory A extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The following mandatory extensions are new for RVA22U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Bit-manipulation instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Hardware performance counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Zihpm was optional in RVA20U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: While the `pause` instruction is a HINT can be implemented as a NOP and hence trivially supported by hardware implementers, its inclusion in the mandatory extension list signifies that software should use the instruction whenever it would make sense and that implementors are expected to exploit this information to optimize hardware execution.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Cache blocks must be 64 bytes in size, naturally aligned in the address space.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: While the general RISC-V specifications are agnostic to cache block size, selecting a common cache block size simplifies the specification and use of the following cache-block extensions within the application processor profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Software does not have to query a discovery mechanism and/or provide dynamic dispatch to the appropriate code.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: We choose 64 bytes at it is effectively an industry standard.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Implementations may use longer cache blocks to reduce tag cost provided they use 64-byte sub-blocks to remain compatible.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Implementations may use shorter cache blocks provided they sequence cache operations across the multiple cache blocks comprising a 64-byte block to remain compatible.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Cache-Block Management Operations. - Cache-Block Prefetch Operations.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: As with other HINTS, the inclusion of prefetches in the mandatory set of extensions indicates that software should generate these instructions where they are expected to be useful, and hardware is expected to exploit that information.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Cache-Block Zero Operations.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: ext:zfhmin[] Half-Precision Floating-point transfer and convert.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The hardware cost for Zfhmin is low, and mandating it avoids adding an option to the profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Data-independent execution time.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Mandating Zkt enables portable libraries for safe basic cryptographic operations.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: It is expected that application processors will naturally have this property and so implementation cost is low, if not zero, in most systems that would support RVA22.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: RVA22U64 has four profile options (Zfh, V, Zkn, Zks):
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Half-Precision Floating-Point.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: A future profile might mandate Zfh.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The smaller vector extensions (Zve32f, Zve32x, Zve64d, Zve64f, Zve64x) are not provided as separately supported profile options.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: The full V extension is specified as the only supported profile option.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Scalar Crypto NIST Algorithms. - Scalar Crypto ShangMi Algorithms.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The smaller component scalar crypto extensions (Zbc, Zbkb, Zbkc, Zbkx, Zknd, Zkne, Zknh, Zksed, Zksh) are not provided as separate options in the profile.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Profile implementers should provide all of the instructions in a given algorithm suite as part of the Zkn or Zks supported options.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Access to the entropy source (Zkr) in a system is usually carefully controlled.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: While the design supports unprivileged access to the entropy source, this is unlikely to be commonly used in an application processor, and so Zkr was not added as a profile option.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: This also means the roll-up Zk was not added as a profile option.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: The Zfinx, Zdinx, Zhinx, Zhinxmin extensions are incompatible with the profile mandates to support the F and D extensions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Recommendations are not strictly mandated but are included to guide implementers making design choices.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Recommendations

sentences.text: Implementations are strongly recommended to raise illegal-instruction exceptions on attempts to execute unimplemented opcodes.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22U64 Profile > RVA22U64 Recommendations

sentences.text: The RVA22S64 profile specifies the ISA features available to a supervisor-mode execution environment in 64-bit applications processors. RVA22S64 is based on privileged architecture version 1.12.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile

sentences.text: RV64I is the mandatory base ISA for RVA22S64 and is little-endian.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The `ecall` instruction operates as per the unprivileged architecture specification.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: An `ecall` in user mode causes a contained trap to supervisor mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: An `ecall` in supervisor mode causes a requested trap to the execution environment.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Base

sentences.text: The following unprivileged extensions are mandatory:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The RVA22S64 mandatory unprivileged extensions include all the mandatory unprivileged extensions in RVA22U64.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Zifencei is mandated as it is the only standard way to support instruction-cache coherence in RVA22 application processors. A new instruction-cache coherence mechanism is under development which might be added as an option in the future.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The following privileged extensions are mandatory:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: *Ss1p12* Privileged Architecture version 1.12.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Ss1p12 supersedes Ss1p11.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: The `satp` mode Bare must be supported.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Page-Based 39-bit Virtual-Memory System.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Page-fault exceptions are raised when a page is accessed when A bit is clear, or written when D bit is clear.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Main memory regions with both the cacheability and coherence PMAs must support hardware page-table reads.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: `stvec.MODE` must be capable of holding the value 0 (Direct).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: When `stvec.MODE=Direct`, `stvec.BASE` must be capable of holding any valid four-byte-aligned address.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: stval must be written with the faulting virtual address for load, store, and instruction page-fault, access-fault, and misaligned exceptions, and for breakpoint exceptions other than those caused by execution of the EBREAK or C.EBREAK instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: For virtual-instruction and illegal-instruction exceptions, stval must be written with the faulting instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: For any hpmcounter that is not read-only zero, the corresponding bit in scounteren must be writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: Fine-Grained Address-Translation Cache Invalidation
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Mandatory Extensions

sentences.text: RVA22S64 has four unprivileged options (Zfh, V, Zkn, Zks) from RVA22U64, and eight privileged options (Sv48, Sv57, Svnapot, Ssu64xl, Sstc, Sscofpmf, Zkr, H).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: The privileged optional extensions are:
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Page-Based 48-bit Virtual-Memory System.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Page-Based 57-bit Virtual-Memory System.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NAPOT Translation Contiguity
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: `sstatus.UXL` must be capable of holding the value 2 (i.e., UXLEN=64 must be supported).
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: supervisor-mode timer interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Sstc was not made mandatory in RVA22S64 as it is a more disruptive change affecting system-level architecture, and will take longer for implementations to adopt.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Count Overflow and Mode-Based Filtering.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Platforms may choose to mandate the presence of Sscofpmf.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: NOTE: Technically, Zk is also a privileged-mode option capturing that Zkr, Zkn, and Zkt are all implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: However, the Zk rollup is less descriptive than specifying the individual extensions explicitly.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: The augmented hypervisor extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Optional Extensions

sentences.text: Implementations are strongly recommended to raise illegal-instruction exceptions when attempting to execute unimplemented opcodes.
sentences.section: Preamble > RVA22 Profiles > RVA22S64 Profile > RVA22S64 Recommendations
